industry: Digital Infrastructure / Bitcoin Mining & AI Data Centers
thesis: Hut 8 is transitioning from a pure-play bitcoin miner to a hybrid digital infrastructure platform. The company has secured $16.8B in contracted AI data center revenue (15-year leases) and raised $7.5B+ in investment-grade project finance for its Texas campuses. Near-term earnings are volatile due to bitcoin treasury mark-to-market accounting, but the AI pivot provides multi-decade revenue visibility. The stock trades at a premium to miners but a discount to data center REITs, reflecting its hybrid nature.

drivers: Beacon Point and River Bend execute on schedule with first AI revenue in 2027
Bitcoin price sustains above $120K, making treasury and mining highly profitable
Additional tenant signings convert 8,375 MW pipeline into contracted revenue
Successful refinancing of mining operations extends cash runway

risks: Construction delays or cost overruns at Beacon Point / River Bend
AI tenant default or renegotiation of $9.8B lease
Bitcoin price crashes below $70K, rendering mining unprofitable
Liquidity crunch forces dilutive equity raise

base_case.assumptions: Beacon Point delivers first revenue in early 2027; Bitcoin trades $90–110K; mining cash flow stabilizes; debt service coverage remains above 1.2x; no major tenant defaults.

catalysts.event: Q3 2026 earnings — potential first AI revenue recognition
Beacon Point Phase 1 operational — first AI hosting revenue

key_risks: Execution risk on first-ever AI data center build at scale
Tenant concentration — single counterparty on $9.8B lease
Bitcoin price volatility impacts quarterly earnings via mark-to-market accounting
High leverage ($7B+ project debt) with delayed revenue ramp
AI demand cyclicality — infrastructure boom may cool by 2027–2028
